reforms.description: Greece has introduced a National Strategy for Trade Facilitation aimed at reducing bureaucratic barriers to international trade, including the establishment of a national 'single window for exports' by the end of 2015.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying export procedures, the strategy aims to enhance the competitiveness of Greek SMEs and integrate them into global value chains, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy was designed to resolve difficulties with trade facilitation and is set to be implemented by the end of 2015.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of trade facilitation processes, which is crucial for enhancing the international competitiveness of Greek firms.
reforms.source_quote: In October 2012, a 2013-15 National Strategy for Trade Facilitation was designed to resolve these difficulties with the establishment of a national 'single window for exports' by end 2015.
